title: nepeta glomerulosa subsp. glomerulosa
intro: Nepeta glomerulosa subsp. glomerulosa, also known as glomerate catmint or Nepeta glomerata, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe, with a long history of traditional use in medicine. The medicinal properties of Nepeta glomerulosa subsp. glomerulosa include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. This plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing anxiety, promoting relaxation, and relieving digestive issues.
